calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-18-2018 Off Calendar

Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Apr-16-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Bryan S. Silverman, Esq. of Lubin Olson & Niewiadomski LLP, T:415.981.0550, for plaintiff/moving party;
Mark S. Posard, Esq. for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on the Order To Show Cause as follows:

Plaintiff Auction Mobility, LLC's application for preliminary injunction is DENIED. Auction's evidence fails to demonstrate a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Auction relies on the declarations of counsel (Mr. Silverman) and the President of Auction (Mr. Brenhouse). The initial and supplemental declarations fail to show that the assertedly defamatory statements quoted at 4:12-5:4 of Auction's reply memorandum are false. At most, Auction shows that there were fewer watch lots on the third auction. (Brenhouse Dec., par. 5.) Auction also fails to show imminent and irreparable harm. The last communication Mr. Berz sent was on March 19, 2018 and there is little evidence to indicate that the asserted "smear campaign" will continue. (Berz Dec., par. 18.) The free speech provisions of the California and United States Constitutions protect defendants' statements and Auction does not demonstrate that a prior restraint of speech would be appropriate in this case. Auction's authorities do not support a prior restraint. Balboa Island Vill. Inn. Inc. v. Lemen (2007) 40 Cal.4th 1141 concerned a permanent injunction after a trial on the merits. The Balboa court explained that "[i]n determining whether an injunction restraining defamation may be issued'it is crucial to distinguish requests for preventative relief prior to trial and post-trial remedies to prevent repetition of statements judicially determined to be defamatory." (Id. at 1158.) People ex rel. Busch v. Projection Room Theater (1976) 17 Cal.3d 42 examined obscene materials and public nuisance law and explained that abatement can occur after a full and fair adversary hearing where the materials are adjudged obscene. DVD Copy Control Ass'n, Inc. v. Bunner (2003) 31 Cal.4th 864 concerned a content-neutral preliminary injunction to protect the plaintiff's property interest and trade secrets. Auction's proposed injunction is content-based to further Auction's viewpoint.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Apr-12-2018 Continued To Apr-16-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

HEARING ON THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE is CONTINUED to April 16, 2018 per the stipulated order filed April 9, 2018.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.
